Title: The Star That Cannot Sit Still

Somewhere in the far reaches of the sky, a distant star has decided that dignity is optional and
spins itself giddy at a pace no respectable celestial body should attempt. Astronomers are
thrilled; I am merely tired on its behalf. Not everything that whirls faster is going somewhere
better, and I suspect this star, like certain acquaintances of mine, will one day wish it had
simply slowed down.

Subject context:
ARTICLE 1: Astronomers spot an unusually fast-spinning distant star, rotating far faster than
typical stars of its class.
